member.name: James McMurdock
member.role: MP for South Basildon and East Thurrock
member.synopsis: James McMurdock is the Independent MP for South Basildon and East Thurrock , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Multi-academy Trusts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of multi-academy trusts on the autonomy of individual schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Multi-academy trusts are partnerships of schools. They are at their best when they combine the collective capacity of the group with the diversity of the component individual schools. We are committed to enhancing the local responsiveness of trusts in a s...

writtenQuestions.heading: Universities: Antisemitism
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what data she holds on the number of antisemitic incidents on university campuses in the academic year 2025/26.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Community Security Trust (CST) collects and publishes data on antisemitic incidents, including those on university campuses. The most recent CST data, set out in its Antisemitic Incidents Report 2025 published February 2026, recorded 78 university‑rel...

writtenQuestions.heading: Further Education: South Basildon and East Thurrock
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of a) staff vacancies and b) turnover on post-16 education in South Basildon and East Thurrock.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Further education (FE) providers are responsible for assessing the impact of staff vacancies and turnover in light of their local circumstances. The government is taking action to strengthen recruitment and retention across the FE workforce and is committ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Further Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what measures are in place to provide support for SEND students in accessing further education.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) code of practice emphasises the importance of supporting young people with SEND to make a successful transition into further education (FE). Planning should begin early and involve the young person, th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Carbon Emissions: Offshoring
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to ensure that progress towards net zero targets is not achieved through the offshoring of carbon-intensive businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Evidence does not suggest the UK’s emissions reductions have been achieved principally through offshoring. The Climate Change Committee’s 2025 Progress Report stated that the UK’s reduction in territorial emissions from 1990 to 2022 significantly outweigh...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero
